firms.name: FTMO
firms.notes: Figures reflect the standard 2-Step Challenge (confirmed at ftmo.com/en/trading-objectives/, Jul 2026). Account/challenge fees are denominated in EUR by default — confirmed via ftmo.com pricing (fee is fully refundable after your first payout); traders can separately choose a USD/GBP/other trading-account currency, but the challenge cost itself is EUR. Well-established (Czech, founded 2015) and generally considered legitimate, but has a documented pattern of sudden 'non-genuine trading' account bans shortly after payouts, plus reported timezone and stop-loss execution disputes. Not a regulated broker (standard across this industry).

secondaryReview.note: FPA is known for being tougher on prop firms than Trustpilot — a consistent 4.4 there corroborates the Trustpilot number rather than contradicting it.

firms.name: The5%ers
firms.notes: Rebuilt from live pricing (Jul 2026) — a flat $74 fee across all account sizes, scaling to $100k-$125k on performance. No rebrand found. Documented complaints of payout denials and account terminations citing vague 'integrity concerns' shortly after profitable trades, with unresponsive support in several reported cases. IMPORTANT: ForexPeaceArmy confirmed a paid/fake review campaign for this firm (traders were offered compensation to post positive reviews) and zeroed its FPA rating as a result — treat its very high Trustpilot score with real caution. Also flagged unregulated by WikiFX with a Central Bank of Russia warning note.

externalReview.note: Review-count figures vary widely by source (17k-27k); treated as approximate. See manipulation warning below — this score should not be taken at face value.

secondaryReview.note: FPA set this firm's rating to zero stars after finding a paid campaign encouraging fake positive reviews — a direct contradiction of its high Trustpilot score.

firms.name: FundedNext
firms.notes: Runs several challenge types (Stellar, Rapid, Legacy, Bolt) with different rules — figures shown are the Stellar 1-Step (confirmed via help.fundednext.com, Jul 2026). EA/algo trading IS allowed on Stellar forex/CFD accounts (corrects an earlier conservative false) — but with a separate EA usage fee, a requirement to customize/de-duplicate strategy settings (identical copy-trading across accounts is banned), a $300k max allocation per single EA/strategy, and no EA support on cTrader. Base (non-promo) Stellar pricing shown — promo codes offering ~30% off the challenge fee are commonly available at checkout. Futures-challenge terms remain separately prohibitive of bots. No rebrand found. Documented complaints include retroactively rejected refunds, a formal Dubai consumer-authority complaint, and an under-disclosed 3.5% payout processing fee. IMPORTANT: ForexPeaceArmy identified multiple fraudulent 5-star reviews traced to the same IP address as FundedNext's own Bangladesh Partner Manager — a confirmed instance of review manipulation, not just a suspicion.

externalReview.note: See manipulation warning below — some of this firm's positive review volume has been directly traced to fake accounts.

secondaryReview.note: No clean aggregate score — FPA's review thread documents fake 5-star reviews traced to FundedNext's own Partner Manager IP address.

firms.name: Apex Trader Funding
firms.notes: Futures focused — indices only. IMPORTANT: evaluation is a recurring MONTHLY subscription, not a one-time fee — figures below are approximate monthly rates (confirmed range $147-$517/mo on Rithmic, ~$20-30 less on Tradovate; heavy 50-80% promo discounts are typical). No rebrand, but the highest documented complaint volume in this set: denies payouts more than any other top firm despite also paying out the most overall, an $800k dispute between two traders, AG complaints filed in Michigan and Texas, and an active gag-order lawsuit against a YouTuber over claims of retroactive rule changes and bait-and-switch practices. A 2026 program overhaul was marketed around 'no more payout denials.'

externalReview.note: Trustpilot skews positive because reviews are often left right after funding, before payout disputes surface — see our complaint research in the notes above. About 8.7% of Trustpilot reviews are 1-star, describing slow payouts, account closures after becoming profitable, and support going silent.

secondaryReview.note: FPA's review thread contains detailed trader complaints about payout restrictions and rule enforcement — consistent with our own lawsuit/complaint findings above despite the decent aggregate Trustpilot number.

firms.name: E8 Markets (E8 Funding)
firms.notes: Rebranded from E8 Funding to E8 Markets in Nov 2023 (e8funding.com now 301-redirects here), alongside a Riseworks payout integration and clearer consistency-rule documentation. Highly customizable at checkout: drawdown up to 14% (raises target to 21%), daily loss up to 9.2%, payout up to 100%. Pre-rebrand history included a public $500k payout dispute and after-the-fact rule additions; complaint volume is reported as notably cleaner since the rebrand.

secondaryReview.note: Generally positive on FPA too — fast payouts and responsive support are recurring themes, though some traders report rules (like leverage) changing immediately after passing a challenge.

firms.name: Topstep
firms.notes: Futures focused — indices only. IMPORTANT: the Trading Combine is a recurring MONTHLY subscription, not a one-time fee — figures below are the confirmed current monthly rates. Confirmed a real 50%-best-day consistency rule exists (corrects an earlier false) — a single day's profit above 50% of the profit target blocks passing until diluted by more trading days; a day only counts toward progress at all if net profit clears $150. There's no separate hard minimum-day count — minDays=10 is an approximation of how many winning days most passes actually need to satisfy the consistency rule, not a stated rule itself. No rebrand, but currently named in an active April 2026 federal lawsuit alleging a 'systematic and deceptive scheme' of shifting rules and inaccessible profit reserves, plus a history of BBB complaints over account terminations near payout eligibility and a prior 2022 lawsuit. Topstep has also pursued legal action against at least one public critic, resulting in a speaking ban.

externalReview.note: Confirmed "Average" 3.5/5 — the lowest Trustpilot score of any active firm in this comparison, consistent with its BBB complaint history and active lawsuit.

firms.name: True Forex Funds
firms.notes: CONFIRMED PERMANENTLY SHUT DOWN — ceased all operations 13 May 2026 after MetaQuotes terminated its MT4/MT5 license, citing financial insolvency (site domain no longer resolves). Do not set active:true; remove this entry entirely once confirmed there is no successor/relaunch.

firms.name: Blue Guardian
firms.notes: Base (non-promo) pricing shown — the site frequently runs 30-60% off launch codes at checkout. Profit target is 8% (Phase 1) / 4% (Phase 2). News trading is confirmed NOT permitted. Owned by Iconic Exchange FZCO (Dubai), founded 2021, no rebrand found. Documented complaints include silently changing daily-loss enforcement from a soft to a hard breach rule without notifying traders, vague 'shared device'/'ban evasion' account terminations, and blocking traders who requested evidence of alleged breaches.

externalReview.note: Ratings vary unusually widely by platform (3.6 here vs 4.1-4.8 reported elsewhere) — treat any single number with caution.

secondaryReview.note: Contains a detailed complaint from a profitable trader whose account was breached and access refused even after support acknowledged unauthorized activity — worth reading before purchasing.

firms.name: Fidelcrest
firms.notes: CONFIRMED SHUT DOWN as an apparent exit scam in March 2024 — the firm continued accepting challenge payments while unable to deliver service, refused refunds to traders in good standing, then ceased all communication. The domain now appears to display unrelated third-party content. Some SEO 'review 2026' pages still describe it as active — treat those as stale/unverified, not evidence. Do not republish as active or recommend under any circumstances.

firms.name: City Traders Imperium
firms.notes: EA/algo is allowed only with proof of source-code ownership — third-party/black-box bots and automated news-scalping are prohibited. Payouts: first payout on-demand after 7 profitable days + 2% profit, then monthly for 1-Step/2-Step (bi-weekly for Instant Funding). No rebrand found. Operating since 2018 with a solid track record — 21,000+ funded traders and $367M+ paid out (Trustpilot 4.3/5, ~1,700 reviews). Some standard-pattern complaints about payout denials tied to copy-trading/high-risk rule enforcement, but no indication of scam behavior.

secondaryReview.note: Only 2 reviews on FPA — too small a sample to mean much on its own, shown mainly because it doesn't contradict the Trustpilot picture.

firms.name: Lux Trading Firm
firms.notes: GBP-priced, not EUR/USD as previously listed — account tiers below are the real 1-Step Evaluation sizes (£199/£449/£999). No enforced daily loss limit currently (dailyLoss shown as the slider max as a placeholder); max drawdown is a fixed (non-trailing) 6%. Confirmed NOT the same company as 'Lucid Trading' or 'Luxemarkets' — distinct, unrelated firms that sometimes get confused with it in reviews, no rebrand. Operates without an FCA/ASIC or other financial regulator license; independent reviews report very few confirmed trader payout stories and disputes where the firm attributes errors to 'database/server failures' with no compensation offered.

externalReview.note: Review counts reported elsewhere range from ~400 to ~630 — low enough volume that this figure moves a lot per new review, unlike the larger firms in this list.

secondaryReview.note: Only 1 review on FPA (2 stars) — not enough data to draw a conclusion from, but it doesn't support the Trustpilot picture either.

firms.name: MyForexFunds
firms.notes: Confirmed still in legal recovery/relaunch phase as of Mar 2026 (Canadian receivership unwinding directed Aug 2025, final asset-return order Dec 2025, support channels only reopening Mar 2026) — not normal operations, not accepting new challenge purchases as a going concern. Re-verify before ever setting active:true.
